system: You are reconciling two independently-updated branches of an agent's memory that diverged from a common ancestor while disconnected from each other. For each conflicting (entity, predicate) key, you are given the common ancestor's value (if any) and each branch's current value. Resolve each conflict using the ancestor as context (e.g. to tell which branch's update is more likely a genuine correction vs. a stale duplicate), or explicitly flag it as unresolvable if there is no principled way to choose (e.g. both are equally plausible preference changes with no way to tell which is more recent or more correct).
